keywords: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS compile-time constant clientId contractTotal gigId freelancerId milestone sum validation OCC FreelancerContractActivated ContractFieldImmutable MilestoneSumMismatch
teachingPoint: T614 MilestoneContractManager uses CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS as a compile-time TypeScript const — never a database lookup or runtime config. ORDER 1 rejects any update attempt touching these fields (ContractFieldImmutable). ORDER 2 validates milestones.reduce(sum) === contractTotal before write. ORDER 3 applies OCC write. This guarantees contract integrity at code level, not policy level.
ironRules: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS = ['clientId','contractTotal','gigId','freelancerId'] as const — compile-time TypeScript constant, NEVER database lookup
ORDER 1: immutable field check — any fieldsToUpdate overlapping CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS returns ContractFieldImmutable immediately, no further processing
ORDER 2: sum validation — milestones must reduce to exactly contractTotal; any mismatch emits MilestoneSumMismatch
ORDER 3: OCC write — storeDocument to CONTRACTS_INDEX; version conflict returns ContractUpdateConflict
storeDocument(CONTRACT_AUDIT_INDEX) MUST complete before enqueue(FreelancerContractActivated) — DNA-8 outbox pattern
